hypothesis: The ∆-learning model's superior performance for MOFs containing transition metals with open-shell d-electrons will degrade significantly when applied to MOFs with lanthanide or actinide f-block metals due to the inability of both GFN1-xTB parameterization and hybrid DFT functionals to adequately capture strong correlation effects in f-electron systems, resulting in larger and less systematic δ-corrections that cannot be reliably learned by the XGBoost model.
